title: Key features of human rights
subtitle: The indices are by [V-Dem](#dod:v-dem), based on expert estimates, and range from 0 to 1 (most rights). Physical integrity includes freedom from government torture and political killings. Private civil liberties include property rights and freedom of movement, religion, and from forced labor. Political civil liberties include freedom of expression and association.
caption:
Key features of human rights

The indices are by V-Dem, based on expert estimates, and range from 0 to 1 (most rights). Physical integrity includes freedom from government torture and political killings. Private civil liberties include property rights and freedom of movement, religion, and from forced labor. Political civil liberties include freedom of expression and association.

This figure presents four line charts tracking human rights indices for Brazil from 1789 to 2025. The vertical axes for all charts range from 0 to 1, where higher values indicate more rights. The "Human rights overall" chart shows a value near 0.15 in the late 18th century, rising to approximately 0.4 around 1830. The line fluctuates between 0.4 and 0.6 through the mid-20th century before climbing sharply after 1980 to a peak above 0.8 around 2010, followed by a decline toward 0.7 by 2025. The "Physical integrity" chart remains flat near 0.2 until roughly 1880, then rises in steps to 0.3 and 0.4. A significant drop occurs around 1960 to near 0, followed by a steep ascent after 1980 to a peak near 0.75, ending with a sharp drop below 0.5. The "Private civil liberties" chart starts near 0.15, rises to 0.25 around 1830, and jumps to 0.55 around 1890. It reaches a plateau near 0.7 in the mid-20th century and peaks above 0.9 around 2010 before declining. The "Political civil liberties" chart begins near 0, rises to 0.7 around 1830, and maintains that level until a drop near 1900. The index falls to near 0 around 1960, then rises rapidly after 1980 to a peak near 0.95, followed by a recent decline to approximately 0.8. All four indices show a similar pattern of sharp increases in the late 20th century and declines in the most recent years shown.
